title: ononis angustissima subsp. longifolia
intro: Ononis angustissima subsp. longifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved wild gorse or broom, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the legume family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Ononis angustissima subsp. longifolia include its ability to relieve menstrual cramps, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its availability and usage may vary due to its limited cultivation and research.
